bio: Hwang Youtaek loves trying new things and building new processes. Debuting younger than most, Hwang pursues a youthful artistic vision — working to tell the stories of those living in contemporary society in forms that feel close to lived reality.

Rooted in theatre, Hwang approaches every audience as unfamiliar and new — through free and diverse theatrical experimentation, unbound by traditional conventions and forms. Beyond the theatre, the work extends to public restrooms as stages, real-time live theatre on social media, and projects like the 'Bus World Tour' that dissolve the boundary between everyday life and the stage — expanding this practice both domestically and internationally.
Beyond theatre, Hwang is deeply engaged in cultural arts programming, arts policy, and youth policy, active across multiple fields.
about.studioDescription: Founded in 2016, THE STRANGER is an artist group that creates new artistic languages through the convergence of technology and genre hybridization. Since the 2018 World Wandering Project (9 countries, 18 cities), the group has pursued trans-linguistic artistic experimentation — expanding through a guest lecture at the National School of Arts in Mexico (2019) and the management of a youth artist residency on Jeju Island (2020–2021). Specialists in direction, media, and technology now collaborate to explore international dialogue through the 'Border' series and beyond, continuing the journey as innovative 'strangers' who cross the boundaries of contemporary art and culture.
